mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and consumer price inflation forces central banks to prioritize disinflation over growth, triggering aggressive monetary tightening that suppresses equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL growth correlates with positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns, with a mean forward return below zero during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation breaching the 4.5 percent annual threshold signals entrenched price pressures that mandate immediate monetary policy tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 70% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 4.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales of automobiles indicate robust consumer discretionary spending and durable goods demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases consistently precede negative 60-day SPX returns, or the correlation coefficient falls below -0.05 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in housing starts exceeds the 10 percent threshold, signaling a severe downturn in construction activity and broader economic weakness that triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances following a housing starts decline exceeding 10 percent.
